seo.defaultTitle: FarmAI Ireland — AI for Irish Farmers

content.officialAdviceBanner: This guide is a starting point. For decisions about grants, animal health, or significant farm investments, always check with your Teagasc advisor or relevant authority.

faq.question: Will AI replace me as a farmer?
faq.answer: No. AI is a tool, not a replacement. It handles data and pattern recognition — the judgment, experience, and knowledge of your land stays with you. Every guide we publish is designed to put you in control, not hand it over to a machine.

faq.question: Do I need to be tech-savvy to use AI tools?
faq.answer: Most of the AI tools we cover work on a smartphone and need little more than a photo or a few taps. We only cover tools that are genuinely accessible — if it needs a computer science degree, we won't recommend it.

faq.question: How much does AI cost for farmers?
faq.answer: Many tools are free or low-cost. Some are built into existing farm management software you may already use. We always include a cost section in our guides so you know what you're getting into before you start.

faq.question: Can I trust the information on FarmAI Ireland?
faq.answer: Every article and guide draws from publicly available information from Teagasc, gov.ie, ICBF, Bord Bia, and other trusted Irish agricultural bodies. We cite our sources in every piece. We're not affiliated with any government body or agri-business.

faq.question: How do I know the information on FarmAI Ireland is accurate?
faq.answer: Every claim is grounded in Teagasc, gov.ie, ICBF, or Bord Bia publications. We cite sources at the bottom of every article. We update articles when official guidance changes. We're not affiliated with any government body or agri-business — our only interest is getting the information right.

faq.question: Is FarmAI Ireland free?
faq.answer: Yes, always. We don't sell anything. The site is funded by occasional sponsorship, which is disclosed clearly in any article where it applies. If it's not disclosed, there's no commercial relationship.

faq.question: Will AI replace farmers?
faq.answer: Short answer: no. Longer answer: that's exactly the kind of question we exist to answer clearly — without the hype and without the fear-mongering. AI handles pattern recognition and data analysis. The judgment calls, the knowledge of your land, the relationships with your animals — that stays with you.

faq.question: How often is new content published?
faq.answer: New articles and guides are published regularly throughout the month. The FarmAI Monthly newsletter rounds up everything once a month — sign up below and you won't miss anything.
